reason: The one-shot v5 preflight recorded its sole attempt at the first Claude profile and failed at credential_attestation after about 179 seconds. V5 did not yet record a durable provider-invocation submarker, so the receipt alone cannot prove whether that subprocess returned; the control flow and elapsed time are consistent with a provider call, and the attempt is conservatively treated as one chargeable Claude call. The managed Glean API-key helper stores OAuth credentials at $HOME/.glean-llm-gateway/credentials.json, while v5 made HOME disposable and incorrectly required a Claude Keychain record. The failed receipt reports no score and the authenticated private state contains zero production assignments. V5 is preserved rather than reset or replayed; v6 requires a fresh cohort, authentication key, private schedule, and credential namespace plus a corrected managed-Glean credential-file contract and a separate no-model authentication bootstrap.
